_purpose: Operational rules for task_edges.json (typed node-to-node relations) so edges can be created and validated WITHOUT relying on example/demo edges. Edge SEMANTICS are owned by SPEC.json link_inference_system.edge_types; this file only codifies the operational/governance rules layered on top.

_history.note: At link_inference_system P0, 42 demo edges were created in a single batch by MockJudge (mock-A/mock-B). These were NOT real judgments.

Removed all 42 demo edges (criterion: jury_models == [mock-A, mock-B]). Extracted rules into this file. Backup: data/task_edges.json.pre_demo_cleanup.bak. One edge remains, edge-4a01d4a126a5 (human_confirmed, dangling) — see known_exceptions.

storage_format.shape: A plain JSON list (array). Do NOT add a top-level metadata/comment key — Store._load expects a list and runs TaskEdge.model_validate on each item.
storage_format.read_write: Do not hand-edit; use the Store API: list_task_edges() / append_task_edge(edge) / save_task_edges(edges). Hand-editing bypasses the confidence invariant validation.
storage_format.git: data/ is gitignored (private). Edge changes are NOT version-controlled by git; rely on dated .bak backups before bulk edits.

id_rules.edge_id: Never author by hand. models.py TaskEdge.id uses default_factory `edge-{uuid4().hex[:12]}` (auto-generated). uuid4 guarantees uniqueness.
id_rules.node_id (= task id): An edge's from_task_id/to_task_id reference task.id values in tasks.json verbatim. task.id follows the hand-authored `<project>-<slug>` convention (e.g. gwangjang-call-interface). Uniqueness is convention-only, so choose new task ids that do not collide with existing ones.
id_rules.no_dangling: Both from and to must reference task.id values that actually exist in tasks.json. Do not create edges pointing at non-existent nodes (dangling).

type_and_direction._authority: SPEC.json link_inference_system.edge_types (including criterion/action). The lines below are only a direction summary.
type_and_direction.blocks: directed (A->B: B requires A's output as input; DAG, no cycles)
type_and_direction.complements: directed (A->B: A feeds B's quality; B still functions without A)
type_and_direction.overlaps_with: undirected (tag overlap > 0.6 AND overlapping output paths/modules; same topic alone is insufficient)
type_and_direction.notify_on_change: undirected (A's public interface/schema is B's input; continuous tracking)
type_and_direction.rule: type and direction must match the _EDGE_DIRECTION mapping in models.py, and self-loops (from == to) are forbidden. Enforced by the TaskEdge validator.

confidence_and_source.formula: confidence = (swap_consistent ? 1.0 : 0.5) * (jury_agreement ? 1.0 : 0.5), except 0.0 when both are false. Only {0.0, 0.5, 1.0} are allowed. If the value is inconsistent with the two booleans, the model validator rejects it.
confidence_and_source.swap_consistent: True only when both orderings (A->B and B->A) yield the same judgment. Disagreement is held back (no edge created).
confidence_and_source.jury_agreement: True when at least 2 distinct REAL models agree.

source.llm_jury: Edge produced by the LLM jury pipeline. Record the real model ids in jury_models.
source.human_confirmed: Confirmed directly by a human. Set jury_models=[] (no judging model).

confidence_and_source.FORBIDDEN_mock: An edge whose jury_models contains 'mock-A'/'mock-B' is MockJudge (heuristic demo) output, not a real judgment. Must NOT be persisted. If found in data/, it is demo residue and should be removed.

lifecycle.status: active is the default. A task targeted by supersedes is preserved as task.status=superseded, not deleted.
lifecycle.verification: drift_check re-judges the same pair monthly and refreshes last_verified_at. Hand-made human_confirmed edges are not subject to drift checks.

how_to_add_without_examples: 1) Pick a candidate pair (via discover/similarity or human judgment). A candidate is not a registration — persist only after explicit approval.
2) Decide the type against SPEC edge_types.criterion. If none clearly fits, do not create an edge (no forced classification).
3) Verify from/to actually exist in tasks.json (no_dangling).

4) human_confirmed: source='human_confirmed', jury_models=[], set swap_consistent/jury_agreement per your confidence (usually both true -> confidence=1.0). llm_jury: only real 2-model pipeline results.
5) Add via Store.append_task_edge(TaskEdge(...)) — leave id to auto-generation.
6) Never persist anything judged by mock-* models.

known_exceptions.issue: Violates no_dangling — both endpoints are currently not seeded in projects/llm_gwangjang/data/tasks.json (non-gwangjang tasks, reseed deferred).
known_exceptions.decision: Kept because it is the only non-demo, human-confirmed edge. Once tasks.json is reseeded (deferred, user said 'later') and the endpoints exist, it normalizes. Until then it is an intended dangling edge.
